tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: My cohort is genuinely welcoming, which makes the invitation hard to reject, but my midterm review is slipping and I'm already tired.
Mina's invitation feels warm, but my fatigue and slipping grades make me hesitate to say yes right now.
Waiting for Mina's response keeps my focus on preserving our connection, even as my fatigue grows heavier.
Mina thanked me warmly, but I let the club night slip away again. My hesitation isn't about her; it's my own fear of friction and exhaustion.
Mina accepted my polite exit, and I feel a pang of guilt that our time slipped away again due to my exhaustion.
The club's warmth feels like a lifeline, yet I'm still hesitant to lean in fully, fearing friction or exhaustion will push me away again.

desires: Not have to explain why I was late or cancel plans, because being unreliable would shatter the fragile safety of this connection.
Feel that the warmth of today is enough to last until tomorrow, rather than just a temporary pause before I have to pull away again.
Keep my head down and finish the midterm prep without anyone noticing how much I'm pushing myself to stay in the moment.

goals.description: Send a brief, vague text to Mina confirming I'm heading out to avoid explaining my absence or revealing my exhaustion.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value demands I don't disappoint her, but my avoidant nature means admitting I skipped study feels like a weakness that could create friction. Deflecting preserves our connection and keeps me from feeling like a burden.

goals.description: Remain at the club social with cohort_3 until the group naturally disperses to ensure we leave on good terms.
goals.rationale: Relationships are weighted highest in my value system. The group just expressed gladness at my presence; pulling away now would risk damaging the closeness I've built, which triggers significant anxiety. The short-term loss of study time is acceptable to protect this long-term social capital.

goals.description: Defer the midterm review until Friday morning; if I collapse tonight trying to study while at the club, it will only reinforce their worry about me, which I must avoid.
goals.rationale: My deliberateness suggests planning for future payoff, but my current stress level and avoidance of conflict make pushing back on the professor's expectations feel risky right now. It's safer to let them assume I'm handling it and focus energy on preserving the group dynamic tonight.

agent.last_reasoning: Mina's vague text confirms my avoidance strategy worked; no friction created. I can now study for 1 hour without guilt, balancing my affiliation need to please her with the academic goal of passing tomorrow.
